question: According to the chart, which statement about the most recent year is justified?

options.A: The most recent year shows a sharp decline in copies sold compared to earlier years.
options.B: The most recent year proves that copies sold will continue to fall in future years.
options.C: None of the above conclusions about the full-year trend are justified, since the latest year only shows data up to June 1.
options.D: The most recent year clearly has the lowest annual copies sold in the period shown.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 2 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (299 copies) directly to full-year values like 1,613 copies creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
